library.description: ملحق SDL3 الرسمي للصوت والمزج وتشغيل الملفات والمسارات الصوتية.

kind.description: استدعاءات تنفذ فعلاً، تغيّر حالة، أو تعيد نتيجة، أو تبني مورداً.

identity.name: MIX_LockMixer
identity.titleArabic: قفل mixer obtaining its الداخلية mutex

summary.actualTranslationArabic: قفل mixer obtaining its الداخلية mutex
summary.actualMeaningArabic: قفل mixer obtaining its الداخلية mutex.
summary.purposeArabic: تستخدم هذه الدالة عندما تحتاج إلى قفل mixer obtaining its الداخلية mutex داخل التطبيق أو داخل مسار التنفيذ المرتبط بها.
summary.whyUseArabic: القيمة الحقيقية هنا هي الأثر التنفيذي الذي يحدثه الاستدعاء أو النتيجة التي يعيدها إلى التطبيق.
summary.carriedValueArabic: القيمة العملية لهذا الاستدعاء تظهر في ناتجه void وفي التغيير الذي يحدثه على المورد أو الحالة المرتبطة به.
summary.sourceDescription: Lock a mixer by obtaining its internal mutex.

parameters.name: mixer
parameters.descriptionArabic: يمثل المعامل mixer من النوع MIX_Mixer * القيمة أو المورد الذي يعتمد عليه هذا الاستدعاء.
parameters.originalDescription: the mixer to lock. May be NULL.

remarks:
While locked, the mixer will not be able to mix more audio or change
its internal state in another thread. Those other threads will block
until the mixer is unlocked again.

Under the hood, this function calls SDL_LockMutex(), so all the same
rules apply: the lock can be recursive, it must be unlocked the same
number of times from the same thread that locked it, etc.

Just about every SDL_mixer API also locks the mixer while
doing its work, as does the SDL audio device thread while actual mixing
is in progress, so basic use of this library never requires the app to
explicitly lock the device to be thread safe. There are two scenarios
where this can be useful, however:

The app has a provided a callback that the mixing thread might call,
and there is some app state that needs to be protected against race
conditions as changes are made and mixing progresses simultaneously. Any
lock can be used for this, but this is a conveniently-available
lock.

The app wants to make multiple, atomic changes to the mix. For
example, to start several tracks at the exact same moment, one would
lock the mixer, call MIX_PlayTrack
multiple times, and then unlock again; all the tracks will start mixing
on the same sample frame.

Each call to this function must be paired with a call to MIX_UnlockMixer from the same thread. It
is safe to lock a mixer multiple times; it remains locked until the
final matching unlock call.

Do not lock the mixer for significant amounts of time, or it can
cause audio dropouts. Just do simple things quickly and unlock
again.
Locking a NULL mixer is a safe no-op.
details.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
